Class Map

java.lang.Object
Assets.Map
public class Map extends Object
Represents the game map/level, loaded from a text file. Manages the grid layout, validates positions, and handles cell updates. Map symbols: - 'X' = Unbreakable wall - '#' = Breakable wall - ' ' = Empty space (walkable) - 'H' = Hero starting position - 'E' = Enemy starting position - 'S' = Snake starting position - 'T' = Treasure location - 'B' = Bomb location

    Display Method: prints the map to the console

    getHeight()
    Gets the height of the map.

    Gets the width of the map.
    boolean
    isValidPos(int x, int y)
    isValidPos : makes sure that the position the player moves to is within the map
    boolean
    isWalkable(int x, int y)
    isWalkable : prevents enemies and heros from walking through walls
    void
    loadFile(String filename)
    FileLoad Method: loads a map from a text file Text file format: first line = height, second line = width, rest = map layout Breakable walls = '#', unbreakable walls = 'X', empty space = ' ', hero start = 'H', enemy start = 'E', treasure = 'T', snake = 'S'
    void
    setCell(int x, int y, char value)
    SetCell : changes the character at x,y

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Map

      public Map()
      Constructor to create an empty map

  • Method Details

    • loadFile

      public void loadFile(String filename)
      FileLoad Method: loads a map from a text file Text file format: first line = height, second line = width, rest = map layout Breakable walls = '#', unbreakable walls = 'X', empty space = ' ', hero start = 'H', enemy start = 'E', treasure = 'T', snake = 'S'
      Parameters:
      filename - The name of the map file to load

    • Display

      public void Display()
      Display Method: prints the map to the console

    • isValidPos

      public boolean isValidPos(int x, int y)
      isValidPos : makes sure that the position the player moves to is within the map
      Parameters:
      x - The x-coordinate to check
      y - The y-coordinate to check
      Returns:
      true if the position is within bounds, false otherwise

    • isWalkable

      public boolean isWalkable(int x, int y)
      isWalkable : prevents enemies and heros from walking through walls
      Parameters:
      x - The x-coordinate to check
      y - The y-coordinate to check
      Returns:
      true if the position is walkable, false otherwise

    • getCell

      public char getCell(int x, int y)
      GetCell : reads the character at the curren position

      Walkable cells include:

      • Empty space (' ')
      • The hero's current position ('H')
      • Enemy positions ('E')
      • Treasure tiles ('T')
      Parameters:
      x - The x-coordinate of the cell
      y - The y-coordinate of the cell
      Returns:
      The character at the specified position, or 'X' if out of bounds

    • setCell

      public void setCell(int x, int y, char value)
      SetCell : changes the character at x,y
      Parameters:
      x - The x-coordinate of the cell to update
      y - The y-coordinate of the cell to update
      value - The new character to set at the specified position

    • getGrid

      public char[][] getGrid()
      Gets the entire map grid. Gets the height and width of the map.
      Returns:
      The 2D character array representing the map

    • getHeight

      public int getHeight()
      Gets the height of the map.
      Returns:
      The height of the map

    • getWidth

      public int getWidth()
      Gets the width of the map.
      Returns:
      The width of the map
